notes.note: Cicero writes to Plancus; the salutation reverses the usual form (sender named first) since Cicero, the more distinguished man, names himself before the recipient by convention.

notes.anchor: the entire fortune of the state is being decided in a single battle
notes.note: Written ca. 30 March 43 BC, this refers to the expected engagement at Mutina; the battles of Forum Gallorum and Mutina were fought on 14 and 21 April 43 BC.

notes.anchor: if we had a consul at Rome
notes.note: Both consuls of 43 BC — Hirtius and Pansa — were with the army at Mutina; in their absence the urban praetor held consular authority at Rome by the custom of the ancestors (mos maiorum).
Plancus and Lepidus both held proconsular commands in Gaul and Spain respectively, making them formal colleagues (conlegae) in imperium; Cicero urges Plancus to keep Lepidus loyal to the Senate.

notes.anchor: The third day before the Kalends of April
notes.note: Ante diem tertium Kalendas Apriles = 30 March 43 BC. The letter was dispatched to Plancus while he was still north of the Rhone.
